seo.title: Building Customer Support with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Llama Stack enables powerful RAG-based interactions with CRM integration, knowledge base retrieval, and automated ticket handling. However, the high-risk nature of handling sensitive customer data and real-world actions demands robust guardrails. Vex integration ensures response accuracy, PII protection, tone consistency, policy compliance, and proper escalation triggers.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Llama Stack customer support agent follows a RAG pattern with memory persistence and tool calling. User queries trigger knowledge base retrieval via vector search, augmented context from CRM systems, and safety filtering before reaching the Llama model. The agent maintains conversation memory across turns and can invoke tools for order lookup or ticket creation.

Core components include the LlamaStack client for agent orchestration, a memory bank for conversation history, tool registry for CRM/ ticketing APIs, and safety shields for content moderation. Post-processing verifies tool responses conform to schemas and agent loops don't exceed resolution thresholds.

This architecture handles complex support scenarios like order status checks, refund requests, and technical troubleshooting while maintaining compliance and safety.

risks.name: Safety shield false positives
risks.description: Benign customer queries about orders blocked as potentially harmful
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering and tone verification with customer support context

risks.name: Agent loop exhaustion
risks.description: Turn limits exceeded without ticket resolution or escalation
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors loop turns and triggers escalation on stalled conversations

risks.name: Tool schema violations
risks.description: CRM API responses don't match expected ticket/order schemas
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs against JSON schemas before agent consumption

risks.name: Memory hallucination
risks.description: Incorrect document associations from knowledge base retrieval
risks.mitigation: Vex validates retrieved context relevance to query and conversation

implementation.title: Initialize Llama Stack Client
implementation.description: Set up the LlamaStack client with customer support tools and memory configuration
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStack
from vex import verify

# Initialize with customer support configuration
stack = LlamaStack(
    model='llama-3.2-90b',
    safety_config={'threshold': 0.7},
    memory_bank='support_tickets',
    tools=[
        {'name': 'create_ticket', 'description': 'Create support ticket', 'schema': {...}},
        {'name': 'lookup_order', 'description': 'Fetch order status', 'schema': {...}}
    ]
)

stack.warmup()

implementation.title: RAG Knowledge Base Retrieval
implementation.description: Implement vector search over support documentation and FAQs
implementation.code:
import numpy as np

def retrieve_context(query, top_k=5):
    # Vector search over knowledge base
    query_embedding = stack.embed(query)
    results = knowledge_base.search(query_embedding, top_k=top_k)
    
    context = []
    for doc in results:
        context.append({
            'content': doc.text,
            'source': doc.metadata['category'],
            'relevance': doc.score
        })
    return context

# Usage in agent turn
context = retrieve_context(user_query)

implementation.title: CRM Tool Integration
implementation.description: Define structured tools for ticket creation and order lookup

implementation.title: Agent Conversation Loop
implementation.description: Main agent loop with memory and tool calling
implementation.code:
async def support_agent(query, conversation_id):
    memory = stack.get_memory(conversation_id)
    context = retrieve_context(query)
    
    messages = memory + [{'role': 'user', 'content': query}]
    
    response = await stack.chat(
        messages=messages,
        context=context,
        max_turns=5,
        tools=True
    )
    
    # Store updated memory
    stack.store_memory(conversation_id, response['messages'])
    return response['content']
